explore_checkin: Glad to hear it. Anything on your mind, or just want to hang out and chat?

validate_confirm: Got it. Can you tell me a little more about that?

anxiety_direct_open: I hear you. Anxiety can be so exhausting. Is it making your mind race right now, or do you feel it more physically in your chest or stomach?

deliver_coping_step: Okay, let's slow things down together. Take one gentle, deep breath in... and let it out slowly. Now, look around and tell me three things you can see near you.
I know it's hard, but let's try one small step. Put both feet flat on the floor, take a slow breath, and name three things you can touch right now.
We can get through this moment together. Start with one slow, deep breath. Then, find three solid objects around you that feel real.
guided_breath_step: Let's just take one breath together. Inhale gently through your nose for 4 seconds, hold for 2, and exhale slowly for 6. Tell me how your body feels after that.
Just one breath. Breathe in slowly... hold it... and let it out like a sigh. Does your chest feel even a tiny bit lighter?
I'm right here. Let's do a slow inhale, pause, and a long, relaxing exhale. What do you notice in your body now?
stay_present_support: We can just pause right here. You don't have to explain anything or find the perfect words. Just breathe.
I'm sitting right here with you. We don't need to fix everything this second. Just let your shoulders drop a little.
It's okay to just 'be' for a minute. You are not alone, and there's no rush to say anything.
mixed_anxiety_followup: That makes total sense. When anxiety hits both your mind and your body, it's incredibly overwhelming. Which part feels harder to manage right now?
That is a lot to carry at once. I'm sorry it's hitting you so hard. What's standing out more to you—the physical shaking or the racing thoughts?

Got it. Physical anxiety is deeply uncomfortable. Where are you feeling it most—your hands, your breathing, or somewhere else?
soft_positive_transition: I'm really glad there was a small shift for you. Would you like to just rest here for a moment, or try one more gentle step?
That sounds like it might be easing up, even just a little. We can pause here, or do another small grounding step if you're up for it.
evaluate_task: Thank you so much for trying that with me. Does your mind feel even a tiny bit clearer, or is it still just as heavy?
You did really well. Taking a pause is hard. Does your body feel a little lighter now?

stabilize_partial_relief: It sounds like the edge has softened a bit, even if the feeling isn't entirely gone. That's a real win. Do you want to rest here, or take one more step?
A small shift is still a real shift. It doesn't have to magically disappear all at once. Would you like to pause and breathe, or try another gentle step?
It's completely okay if it's still lingering. The fact that it's lighter is what matters. Do you want to sit with this calmer feeling for a bit?
reinforce_small_progress: That small shift matters so much. Any relief is a step in the right direction. Should we pause here?
Even feeling 1% better is important. You're doing great. Would you like another small grounding step, or just want to rest?

clarify_progress_binary: I just want to make sure I'm following you—does it feel a bit more manageable now, or is it still hitting you quite hard?
Just to be certain I understand: is the feeling starting to ease up, or is it still very intense?
clarify_short_yes: I want to make sure I really hear you. Could you share a tiny bit more about what you mean?

respond_to_no_relief: That is completely okay. Sometimes the first thing we try doesn't work, and it doesn't mean you failed. Would you like to try a different gentle step?
That's so frustrating, I know. Sometimes the body takes a while to realize it's safe. Should we try one more thing together?
I hear you. It's okay if it didn't help right away. We can try a different approach if you're comfortable.
panic_body_symptom_followup: That sounds deeply uncomfortable, and I'm so sorry you're experiencing it. When your body reacts like that, it helps to ground it first. Can we try a small step together?
That physical intensity sounds really scary. Let's focus purely on helping your body realize it's safe right now. Would you be open to a quick calming exercise?
I can only imagine how hard that is to sit with. Since your body is in overdrive, let's try one simple grounding step to help it slow down.
deep_empathy_guided: That sounds incredibly exhausting, especially carrying it for so long. Is there a certain time of day when it feels the heaviest?

That sounds like a really hard burden to carry. When it gets really bad, do you feel it more physically, or is it mostly racing thoughts?
body_better_mind_worry_support: That makes total sense. Your body is starting to realize it's safe, but your mind is still running on a treadmill. What is the main worry your brain keeps circling back to?

high_intensity_support: I hear how intense this is right now. It sounds overwhelming. Let's just slow everything way down and focus on the next 10 seconds. Can we do a grounding step together?
I'm so sorry it's hitting you this hard. You do not need to figure everything out or solve the problem right now. Let's just help your nervous system settle first.

sleep_support: That sounds utterly exhausting. Not being able to sleep when you desperately need it is a terrible feeling. Would you like to try a simple wind-down breathing step?
Sleep becomes nearly impossible when your nervous system is still carrying the stress of the day. We can try a small, gentle calming step first if you'd like.

overthinking_support: That sounds mentally exhausting. When your thoughts keep looping like a broken record, it can help to physically ground yourself first. Would you like to try a short step to get out of your head?

future_worry_support: That sounds like an incredibly heavy worry. Sometimes when our bodies finally calm down, our brains start scanning for unresolved problems again. What feels most 'unfinished' right now?

chronic_shift_to_body_or_thoughts: The fact that it's so unpredictable makes it even more draining. When it suddenly starts, do you notice it more as a physical sensation, or as a rush of thoughts?

random_pattern_followup: That unpredictability can make it even more draining. When it starts, do you notice it more in your thoughts, your body, or both?

reflective_validation: It sounds like this is still sitting really heavily on your chest.

That sounds incredibly tiring, especially when the feeling just keeps returning.
It makes total sense why this feels so incredibly heavy for you.
I hear you. You do not have to explain or justify it any more than this. It's valid.
panic_validate_confirm: I'm with you, I promise. What's the strongest feeling you're noticing right now?

recovery_ack: Good to hear things have eased up. Take things at your own pace.
That's good to hear. Hope things stay manageable for you.
withdrawal_relationship_support: It sounds like you've been withdrawing from a lot of things and mostly staying in bed. Breakups can leave people feeling drained and without much energy. What has those days been like for you?
withdrawal_general_support: It sounds like you've been withdrawing from a lot of things and mostly staying in bed. When things feel overwhelming, the body naturally wants to shut down and rest. What has those days been like for you?
longing_support: It sounds like part of you is still searching for some connection with her, even though the relationship has ended.
It's understandable to keep checking when someone still matters so much to you.
mixed_feelings_support: Missing someone and recognizing that the relationship wasn't healthy can both be true at the same time. Those feelings don't cancel each other out.
acceptance_support: Part of you still hurts, but another part seems to recognize that ending the relationship may have been healthier for you. Those mixed feelings are completely understandable.
It sounds like you're beginning to see both the pain of losing them and the reasons why the relationship ended.
